chapter_title.en: The Virtue of Mosques, Their Sanctity and the Reward for Those Who Pray in Them

parts.text: 706 - وَقَالَ أَبُو جَعْفَرٍ عَلَيْهِ اَلسَّلاَمُ: «أَوَّلُ مَا يَبْدَأُ بِهِ قَائِمُنَا سُقُوفُ اَلْمَسَاجِدِ فَي

text: ُكَسِّرُهَا وَ يَأْمُرُ بِهَا فَيُجْعَلُ عَرِيشاً كَعَرِيشِ مُوسَى.

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.706 - Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as) said:
"The first thing our Qa'im (the awaited savior Imam Muhammad ibn Hassan Al-Mahdi (as) ) will do is to break the roofs of the mosques and order them to be made as an open canopy (like the canopy of Musa (as))”.
